# The Ritual Journal Collective

> An independent editorial publication exploring grief rituals, polyvagal theory, IFS (Internal Family Systems), and somatic healing.

The Ritual Journal Collective covers the intersection of ancient ritual practice and modern nervous system science. We focus on practical, evidence-informed approaches to grief processing that draw on:

- **Polyvagal theory** (Porges): How the autonomic nervous system processes and stores grief
- **IFS (Internal Family Systems)**: Parts-based approaches to unresolved loss
- **Somatic therapy**: Body-centered practices for releasing stored grief
- **Ritual practice**: Ancient and contemporary ceremonial frameworks for honoring loss

## Topic Clusters

- Grief Rituals (stone rituals, releasing ceremonies, lunar cycles, ancestral grief)
- Polyvagal Theory + Grief (dorsal vagal shutdown, co-regulation, vagal toning, window of tolerance)
- IFS + Grief (exiles, unburdening, self-guided parts work)
- Emotional Release Practices (somatic release, breathwork, movement, writing rituals)
- Morning Rituals for Grief
- Healing Rituals (water, fire, sound, solo ceremony)

## Authors

- **Sable March** — Somatic Grief Practitioner & Ritual Guide
- **Eden Voss** — Trauma-Informed Wellness Writer
- **M. Ellis** — Psychology Researcher & Grief Writer

## Editorial Standards

All articles cite peer-reviewed research (PubMed, APA, NIH). Content is educational — not a substitute for professional mental health care.
